title: Tests Hub
description: Start from a clearer question or category and choose the right FermatMind assessment more quickly.

seo.title: Tests Hub
seo.description: Start from a clearer question or category and choose the right FermatMind assessment more quickly.

hero.title: Start from a clearer question and find the assessment that fits.
hero.body: A curated set of assessment entry points for self-understanding, learning, career direction, relationships, and collaboration.
hero.previewTitle: Start with the question, then move into the right assessment.
hero.previewBody: Use a clearer starting surface for career direction, personality structure, state, and ability signals.

quickStart.title: Start from the question that matters most right now.
quickStart.body: Name the question first, then move into the version that fits best.

items.title: What career direction fits me best?
items.description: Start with interest, work-style, and capability signals before deciding which path deserves deeper effort.

items.title: What does my personality structure look like?
items.description: Build a decision-ready self-understanding baseline through personality style and stable trait structure.

items.title: How is my current emotional state?
items.description: If your main concern is recent fluctuation or warning signals, state-oriented tests should come first.

items.title: Where are my cognitive strengths?
items.description: Use the ability entry when you want a clearer read on reasoning performance and baseline capability.

items.title: How do I show up in collaboration and relationships?
items.description: Combine emotional skills and preference patterns to read how you tend to communicate, coordinate, and respond.

families.title: Continue through the family that best matches your question.
families.body: If you know the broad topic but not the exact test, this is the clearer way forward.

items.title: Personality & Style
items.description: Best for building a stable map of preferences, traits, and long-horizon tendencies.

tests.title: MBTI Personality Test
tests.description: When you want a quick read on personality style, preference patterns, and collaboration tendencies.
tests.outputLabel: Type profile, preference map, and scenario interpretation

tests.title: Big Five Personality Test
tests.description: When you need a more dimensional view of how stable traits shape work, stress, and relationships.
tests.outputLabel: Five-factor trait profile with strengths and application notes

tests.title: EQ Test
tests.description: When you want to review emotional regulation, communication, and collaboration skills.
tests.outputLabel: Emotional skill profile, communication cues, and growth direction

items.title: Career & Direction
items.description: Best for combining interest, work style, and capability signals into career direction decisions.

tests.title: Holland Career Interest Test (RIASEC)
tests.description: When you need a first-pass read on interest patterns, role direction, and industry fit.
tests.outputLabel: RIASEC profile and top interest codes

tests.title: Big Five Personality Test
tests.description: When you need a more dimensional view of how stable traits shape work, stress, and relationships.
tests.outputLabel: Five-factor trait profile with strengths and application notes

tests.title: MBTI Personality Test
tests.description: When you want a quick read on personality style, preference patterns, and collaboration tendencies.
tests.outputLabel: Type profile, preference map, and scenario interpretation

items.title: Emotion & State
items.description: Best for recent fluctuation, warning signals, and deciding whether further support is needed.

tests.title: Depression and Anxiety Assessment
tests.description: When you want a fuller snapshot across both depression and anxiety dimensions.
tests.outputLabel: Depression and anxiety state reference

items.title: Cognition & Ability
items.description: Best for reasoning performance, baseline capability, and cognitive strength signals.

tests.title: IQ Test
tests.description: When you want a read on reasoning performance, pattern recognition, and cognitive strengths.
tests.outputLabel: Ability baseline, reasoning profile, and reference notes

items.title: Relationship & Collaboration
items.description: Best for reading communication style, emotional handling, and collaboration friction.

tests.title: EQ Test
tests.description: When you want to review emotional regulation, communication, and collaboration skills.
tests.outputLabel: Emotional skill profile, communication cues, and growth direction

tests.title: MBTI Personality Test
tests.description: When you want a quick read on personality style, preference patterns, and collaboration tendencies.
tests.outputLabel: Type profile, preference map, and scenario interpretation

howToChoose.title: Choose based on the decision you need to make.
howToChoose.body: Only keep the rules you need to make the next decision.

items.title: For a first pass, start with personality or career direction
items.description: Those entries work best as first-pass routes because they help build the broader decision frame.

items.title: If the concern is recent fluctuation, start with emotion and state
items.description: Those tests are better for “what is happening lately?” than “who am I in general?”

items.title: If the goal is strengths and capability, start with cognition and ability
items.description: That route is more useful for learning strategy, problem solving, and baseline ability checks.

items.title: Results support judgment. They do not define a person.
items.body: Use reports to structure discussion, clarify tendencies, and decide the next move instead of treating them as final labels.

items.title: You can start anonymously
items.body: You can start from the question and the right test version first, then decide later whether to share more information.

items.title: Not a substitute for medical, legal, or diagnostic advice
items.body: State-oriented results are structured references, not formal diagnosis, treatment, or legal guidance.

items.title: Results should be reviewed in context
items.body: The strongest use cases are learning plans, career conversations, collaboration review, and recurring reflection.

resources.title: Read the three most useful resources before you go deeper.
resources.body: Three short reads that make the next choice easier.

items.title: How to Find a Better-Fit Career Direction
items.description: Bring interest, ability, and real constraints into one decision frame instead of relying on a single instinct.

items.title: MBTI Basics
items.description: Understand what MBTI helps with and where its interpretation boundary should stop.

items.title: Big Five Tool Guide
items.description: A clearer pre-read if you prefer dimensional interpretation over type-based language.

finalCta.title: Start from the better question first, then decide how deep to go.
finalCta.body: If you are not sure which test to take yet, start with the question entry or the category hubs first.
